items.title: Add GnuCash Python bindings debugging guide and utilities
items.body:
- Add docs/DEBUGGING_GNUCASH_BINDINGS.md with practical workflow for debugging GnuCash Python SWIG vs ctypes issues across platforms
- Add infrastructure/gnucash/engine.py utilities:
  - GList structure class for safe traversal of GnuCash C API lists
  - iterate_glist() to replace raw pointer arithmetic with safe iteration
  - safe_ctypes_string() for null-safe string decoding
  - verify_ctypes_functions() for runtime validation of required C functions
- Update use_cases/export_business_objects.py to use new utilities:
  - Fix callback signature mismatch that prevented tax table export
  - Replace manual GList traversal with iterate_glist()
  - Replace manual string decoding with safe_ctypes_string()
- Update services/invoice_renderer.py similarly for consistency
- Update CLAUDE.md with hard-won platform findings:
  - Always set argtypes for 64-bit pointer safety
  - RTLD_LOCAL vs RTLD_GLOBAL differences (Debian vs Ubuntu)
  - Reading vs writing asymmetry in GnuCash bindings reliability
  - Pointer lifetime rules when mixing SWIG and ctypes

These changes address platform-specific bugs discovered during comprehensive testing across all 6 supported Linux distributions (Debian 11/12/13, Ubuntu 20/22/24). The pragmatic "try SWIG first, then ctypes" workflow is now documented and supported by reusable utilities that make ctypes usage safer and more maintainable.

## Summary

Two commits on `swig-2.0.0-alpha.1` net out to:

items.title: refactor(build): remove C++ taglib adapter
items.body:
### Description

This PR removes the CGO-based C++ TagLib adapter (`adapters/taglib/`) and all the cross-taglib build infrastructure that came with it. The WASM-based `adapters/gotaglib/` adapter (already present) becomes the sole metadata extractor. Because `gotaglib` registers itself under the name `"taglib"`, existing installations with `ND_SCANNER_EXTRACTOR=taglib` (the default) keep working transparently — no configuration change is required.

**What's removed:**
- `adapters/taglib/` — the entire CPP/CGO wrapper (~1400 lines)
- `.github/actions/download-taglib/` — the download composite action
- All `CROSS_TAGLIB_VERSION` / `CGO_CFLAGS_ALLOW` / `pkg-config --define-prefix` references from `Dockerfile`, `.devcontainer/Dockerfile`, `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`, `.github/workflows/pipeline.yml`, and `Makefile`
- The `taglib-build` stage and its mount/env exports in `Dockerfile` (the existing glibc `build` stage is kept intact)
- The `_ "github.com/navidrome/navidrome/adapters/taglib"` import from `cmd/root.go` and `cmd/wire_gen.go`

**Defensive fix included:** `core/storage/local/local.go` no longer calls `log.Fatal` when the configured extractor is not registered — it now warns and falls back to `consts.DefaultScannerExtractor` ("taglib", which resolves to gotaglib after this PR). This protects users whose config still references a removed/unknown extractor name (e.g. a stale `ffmpeg` value) from a hard startup crash.

**Why:** The CPP TagLib adapter required a pre-built cross-compiled taglib distribution, a dedicated GitHub action to download it, `--define-prefix` CGO flag allowlisting, and a separate `taglib-build` Docker stage. With gotaglib (WASM) mature and already the default extractor, the CPP adapter is redundant build complexity.

### Related Issues

None.

### Type of Change
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Documentation update
- [x] Refactor
- [ ] Other (please describe):

### Checklist
- [x] My code follows the project's coding style
- [x] I have tested the changes locally
- [ ] I have added or updated documentation as needed
- [x] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works (or explain why not)
- [x] All existing and new tests pass

_No new tests: this PR is a pure deletion of a redundant adapter; the existing `core/storage/local` and `adapters/gotaglib` test suites cover the remaining extractor path, and they pass._

### How to Test

**1. Default install (existing taglib config keeps working):**
1. Build and run: `make build && ./navidrome`
2. Scan a music library with a variety of formats (mp3, flac, ogg, m4a, etc.)
3. Verify metadata (title, artist, album, track/disc, replay gain, cover art) is extracted correctly — it should be identical to master, since gotaglib was already the default.

**2. Stale extractor config no longer crashes:**
1. Set `ND_SCANNER_EXTRACTOR=ffmpeg` (or any unknown value) in env or `navidrome.toml`
2. Start navidrome
3. Confirm it no longer exits with `FATAL` — instead it logs a WARN `"Extractor not found, using default"` and falls back to `taglib` (gotaglib), and scanning works.

**3. Docker image build:**
1. `make IMAGE_PLATFORMS=linux/amd64 DOCKER_TAG=navidrome:test docker-image`
2. Verify build completes without referencing `taglib-build`, `CROSS_TAGLIB_VERSION`, or `cross-taglib` downloads.
3. Run the container, scan a small library, verify metadata extraction works.

**4. Standalone binary via docker buildx:**
1. `docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64 --output ./binaries --target binary .`
2. Run the produced binary outside Docker and scan a library.

### Screenshots / Demos

Not applicable — no UI changes.

### Additional Notes

- **No behavior change for the default config.** `gotaglib` registers under the name `"taglib"`, so the default `ND_SCANNER_EXTRACTOR=taglib` keeps resolving to a valid extractor. No migration needed.
- **Net diff:** 17 files changed, ~3 insertions / ~1440 deletions across both commits.
- **Docker build simplification:** the `Dockerfile` loses the `taglib-build` stage entirely and the glibc `build` stage no longer needs `PKG_CONFIG_PATH`, `CGO_CFLAGS_ALLOW`, or the taglib mount.
- **CI simplification:** `.github/workflows/pipeline.yml` no longer needs the `Download TagLib` step in lint or test jobs, nor the `pkg-config --define-prefix --cflags --libs taglib` debug line before tests.

items.title: Add ctypes Airspy wrapper module for Windows
items.body:
### Motivation
- Provide a small, pure-Python `ctypes` wrapper around `airspy.dll` so the Airspy device can be opened, configured, and used from Python on Windows.
- Expose the core control functions and an RX callback that collects interleaved int16 I/Q samples into NumPy for downstream processing.

### Description
- Added `airspy_wrapper.py` which loads `airspy.dll` from the module directory via `ctypes.CDLL` and resolves the DLL path using `Path(__file__).resolve().parent / "airspy.dll"`.
- Declared an `AirspyTransfer` `ctypes.Structure` and `AIRSPY_RX_CALLBACK` `CFUNCTYPE`, and configured `argtypes`/`restype` for `airspy_open`, `airspy_close`, `airspy_set_sample_rate`, `airspy_set_freq`, `airspy_set_lna_gain`, `airspy_set_vga_gain`, `airspy_start_rx`, and `airspy_stop_rx`.
- Implemented an `AirSpy` class with `open()`, `close()`, `set_frequency(hz)`, `set_sample_rate(rate)`, `start()`, and `stop()` methods and added `set_lna_gain()`/`set_vga_gain()` helpers; all DLL calls check return codes and raise `AirSpyError` on non-zero responses.
- Implemented the RX callback to copy incoming interleaved `int16` samples, convert them to `numpy` complex64 (I + 1j*Q), buffer chunks during `start()`, and return a concatenated NumPy array from `stop()`; included a runnable usage example under `if __name__ == "__main__":`.

### Testing
- Ran `python -m py_compile airspy_wrapper.py` which completed without errors.

items.title: Upgrade to latest swig 4.1.1

labels.name: build
labels.description: Build system or compilation enhancement

items.body:
* **Tickets addressed:** bsk-1201
* **Review:** By commit
* **Merge strategy:** Merge (no squash)

## Description

This PR updates Basilisk test and build infrastructure for cleaner Python 3.14 support.

First, the branch updates SWIG dependency handling so Basilisk supports the latest working SWIG release.  Local development requirements now allow current SWIG versions while explicitly excluding `swig==4.4.0`, which produced compile failures in testing.  SWIG 4.4.1 was verified with a clean Basilisk build and unit test run.  The canary CI requirements continue to use the latest available SWIG so future compatibility issues are detected early.

Second, this PR removes obsolete SWIG runtime warning suppression that was previously hiding Python warnings such as `swigvarlink has no __module__ attribute`.  With SWIG 4.4.1, those warnings no longer appear in the clean build/test path, so the suppression is no longer needed.

Finally, this PR cleans up Python test-suite `ResourceWarning` messages observed when running `pytest -n auto` from `src`.  The warnings were traced to pytest infrastructure rather than Basilisk simulation code.  The SPICE thread-safety test now closes multiprocessing queue and process resources explicitly.  The pytest configuration also closes the optional `reportconf.py` file handle after loading it.

The remaining socket warnings were isolated to the interaction between `pytest-xdist` and `pytest-rerunfailures` 16.1.  Since there is not yet a newer released `pytest-rerunfailures` version that fixes this cleanup path, `src/conftest.py` now performs a narrow local cleanup: it closes the plugin-owned rerun status socket during pytest shutdown and wraps the plugin’s server-side connection handler so accepted sockets are closed when each handler exits.

The commits are organized by infrastructure topic: SWIG version support, removal of obsolete SWIG warning suppression, SPICE multiprocessing cleanup, pytest file-handle cleanup, and pytest-rerunfailures socket cleanup.

## Verification

SWIG 4.4.1 was verified with a clean Basilisk build.  The previous SWIG runtime warnings were not observed in the clean build log, and the unit tests completed successfully.  SWIG 4.4.0 was tested separately and produced compile failures, so that specific version is excluded from the pinned development dependency range.

Validated the ResourceWarning cleanup with warning tracing enabled:

```bash
env PYTHONTRACEMALLOC=25 PYTHONWARNINGS=always::ResourceWarning .venv/bin/pytest -q src/simulation/environment/spiceInterface/_UnitTest/test_spiceThreadSafety.py
```

Also verified the full parallel test suite from src:

pytest -n auto
The full run completed without the previously reported unclosed socket ResourceWarning.

No new tests were added because these changes update build/test infrastructure cleanup behavior rather than Basilisk runtime behavior. Existing build and test coverage was used to confirm the changes.

## Documentation
No user-facing Basilisk documentation was invalidated. This change affects developer/test infrastructure and SWIG dependency compatibility.

Reviewers should check the requirements updates, the removed SWIG warning suppression, src/conftest.py for the local pytest-rerunfailures socket cleanup shim, and src/simulation/environment/spiceInterface/_UnitTest/test_spiceThreadSafety.py for multiprocessing queue/process cleanup behavior.

## Future work
If pytest-rerunfailures releases an upstream fix for closing its xdist coordination sockets, the local cleanup shim in src/conftest.py can be removed after updating the pinned dependency range.

items.title: Derive ctypes/LLVM types from layout struct format
items.body:
## Summary
Stacked on #149 (register-passable aggregates).

The layout's `struct.Struct` format IS the ABI. This PR makes the codegen derive both LLVM IR type strings and ctypes types mechanically from that format, eliminating the parallel lookup tables and the three-way switch between scalars, aggregates, and pointer-passed values.

## Key changes

**`dgen/layout.py`**: `Record.struct` now concatenates field formats (e.g. `"qP"` for `{i64, ptr}`) instead of raw bytes (`"16s"`). This means the struct format directly describes the ABI for all layout types.

**`dgen/llvm/codegen.py`**:
- `_struct_fields(fmt)` parses a struct format string into individual field characters
- `_llvm_type(layout)` and `_ctype(layout)` derive types from the parsed fields
- The `call()` FFI bridge simplifies: args → `_ctype(layout).from_buffer_copy(mem.buffer)`, results → `bytes(ctypes_value)`
- Removed: `_FMT_LLVM`, `_FMT_CTYPE`, `_aggregate_ctype`, `_to_raw_arg`, `_from_raw_result`

**Net -23 lines.**

## Test plan
- [x] `pytest test/ examples/ --ignore=examples/dcc` — 624 passing, no changes

items.title: Prevent ctypes/X11 pointer mismatch causing Python helper segfault
items.body:
Le helper Python pouvait crasher par segmentation fault lors des commandes X11 (notamment `check-display`) au lieu de remonter une erreur propre. Le problème venait de signatures `ctypes` incomplètes sur des fonctions natives X11.

- **Root cause: signatures natives incomplètes**
  - `ctypes` utilisait des types par défaut pour certains symboles X11.
  - Sur plateformes 64-bit, cela peut tronquer des pointeurs/arguments et provoquer un segfault.

- **Hardening des bindings X11**
  - Ajout des `argtypes` manquants:
    - `XKeysymToKeycode(ctypes.c_void_p, ctypes.c_ulong)`
    - `XStringToKeysym(ctypes.c_char_p)`
  - Ajout des `restype` explicites:
    - `XFlush -> ctypes.c_int`
    - `XCloseDisplay -> ctypes.c_int`

- **Comportement attendu après changement**
  - Les erreurs d’accès display restent des erreurs applicatives gérées (`Cannot open display ...`), sans crash process.

```python
self.X11.XKeysymToKeycode.argtypes = [ctypes.c_void_p, ctypes.c_ulong]
self.X11.XStringToKeysym.argtypes = [ctypes.c_char_p]
self.X11.XFlush.restype = ctypes.c_int
self.X11.XCloseDisplay.restype = ctypes.c_int
```

items.title: Add Python 3 importsym script and regenerate symbols.i
items.body:
The existing `importsym.py` has several issues:
- Requires Python 2 (uses `print` statements, `dict.has_key()`, etc.)
- Depends on `pycparser` external package with hardcoded install paths
- Requires pycparser's `fake_libc_include` headers to be present
- Has a bug: references undefined variable `k` instead of `name` in warning message

This PR adds `importsym2.py`, a Python 3 rewrite that uses `cpp` (C preprocessor) + regex instead of `pycparser`, eliminating all external dependencies. The original `importsym.py` is kept for reference.

Also:
- Remove `pjmedia/tonegen.h` from `symbols.lst` — these are struct types (not enums) that were never successfully extracted and are not referenced in the SWIG bindings.
- Regenerate `symbols.i` with current header definitions (picks up new enum values added since last generation).

Usage: `python3 importsym2.py` (from `pjsip-apps/src/swig/`)

### Test plan

- [ ] Run `python3 importsym2.py` and verify `symbols.i` is generated with all symbols
- [ ] SWIG bindings build successfully with the regenerated `symbols.i`

items.title: Fix Windows drag-and-drop ctypes handle overflow
items.body:
### Motivation
- Prevent an `OverflowError: int too long to convert` that occurs when the `WM_DROPFILES` `wParam` is passed directly to shell32 APIs via ctypes. 
- Ensure the shell drag handle is used with the correct native type and always released via `DragFinish` to avoid resource or API misuse.

### Description
- Declare explicit ctypes signatures for `shell32.DragQueryFileW` and `shell32.DragFinish` by setting `restype` and `argtypes` so parameters and return values use native types. 
- Convert the incoming `drop_handle`/`wParam` to `wintypes.HANDLE` before calling `DragQueryFileW` and `DragFinish`. 
- Wrap the loop that reads dropped paths in a `try/finally` and call `DragFinish(handle)` in `finally` so the drop handle is always finished even on error. 
- Update calls to use the new `handle` variable when querying file names.

### Testing
- Ran `python -m py_compile app.py` which completed successfully.

items.title: Feature/python rewrite of debthin scripts
items.body:
# Pipeline Refactor: Python Orchestration and HTTP/2 Fetch

This pull request implements the transition from shell script orchestration (`fetch.sh`, `filter.sh`, `sign_all.sh`, and `validate.sh`) to Python implementations, alongside explicit performance improvements in the network request wrapper.

## Key Changes

### 1. HTTP/2 with httpx (`fetch.py`)
- Replaced the standard library `urllib` and `ThreadPoolExecutor` implementation with an asynchronous event loop (`asyncio`).
- Integrated `httpx.AsyncClient` configured with `http2=True`.
- Replaced OS-level thread constraints with an `asyncio.Semaphore` bound (via `PARALLEL=8`) to manage concurrent connections over multiplexed streams.
- Retained the `If-Modified-Since` parsing against `.tmp_cache/` timestamps.

### 2. Virtual Environment (`.venv`)
- Added `.venv` as an explicit build target in the `Makefile`. Triggered automatically when `requirements.txt` changes.
- Added `httpx[http2]` and `boto3` to `requirements.txt`.
- Set `PYTHON_CMD := $(ROOT_DIR)/.venv/bin/python3` universally across the `Makefile`. 

### 3. Signature and Validation Enhancements (`sign_all.py` & `validate.py`)
- Replaced `sign_all.sh` and `validate.sh` with Python multiprocessing.
- `sign_all.py` coordinates detached `gpg` signature generation and concurrent `hashlib` SHA256 computations using `ProcessPoolExecutor`.
- `validate.py` executes structural validation without relying on deep bash sub-shells.

### 4. Obsolete Shell Script Removal
- Deleted obsolete orchestrator wrappers (`fetch.sh`, `filter.sh`, `sign_all.sh`, and `validate.sh`).
- The `Makefile` relies on Python logic to parse `config.json` instead of using external `jq` and `xargs` processing.

## Performance Impact
- A full execution utilizing an active `.tmp_cache/` completes a `NO_UPLOAD=1` (`fetch`, `filter`, `headless`, `sign`, `stage`, `validate`) pass in ~21.8 seconds.

items.title: fix(python_plugins_loader): suppress -Wconversion for SWIG-generated code

labels.name: downstream
labels.description: Also reported in Jira or Bugzilla

items.body:
The SWIG-generated swigpyrun.h contains an implicit size_t to int narrowing in SWIG_Hash():
```c
  rem -= sizeof(unsigned int);
```
This triggers -Werror=conversion with GCC 16 (not Clang). The bug is fixed in SWIG master (https://github.com/swig/swig/pull/3287), but unfortunately, not in any released SWIG version at the time of writing (see
https://github.com/swig/swig/pull/3287/changes/296fe5e4325795e8fea933341c20b73566cffc87, 4.4.1 narrowly missed it).

This warning was previously masked by a leaked -Wconversion diagnostic suppression in fmt < 12 (https://github.com/fmtlib/fmt/pull/4588). fmt 12 properly scoped the suppression, exposing this pre-existing SWIG issue.

Add -Wno-conversion to the existing warning suppression list for the python_plugins_loader target, consistent with the existing approach of suppressing warnings in SWIG-generated code that we don't control.

We can revert this change once the downstream packaging picks up the SWIG fix.

items.title: Implement C-based DSA core and integrate via thin ctypes adapter
items.body:
### Motivation
- Move hot-path strategy ordering and simulation logic into a small, native C runtime for clearer separation and potential performance improvements while keeping behaviour identical.\n- Provide a minimal, low-risk integration that preserves existing Python code paths when the native library is unavailable.\n
### Description
- Added a new C core under `c_core/` with public header `include/scheduler_dsa.h`, implementation `src/scheduler_dsa.c`, a `Makefile`, and unit test `tests/test_scheduler_dsa.c`.\n- Implemented functions `ccore_order_indices`, `ccore_average_wait`, and `ccore_simulate_strategy` which perform ordering for Priority/FIFO/LIFO strategies and compute average wait time.\n- Added a thin Python `ctypes` adapter at `scheduler/c_core_adapter.py` which loads `c_core/libscheduler_dsa.so` and falls back to the original Python implementation if the shared library is missing or returns an error.\n- Integrated the adapter into the scheduler runtime by delegating ordering from `Scheduler.get_next_task()` and `Scheduler.get_all_tasks()` to `c_order_tasks`, and delegating simulation via `scheduler/data_structures.py` to the adapter; updated `README.md` with build/test/run instructions and integration notes.\n
### Testing
- Ran C unit tests with `make -C c_core test` which produced `PASS: c_core scheduler DSA tests`.\n- Built the shared library with `make -C c_core` which completed without errors.\n- Ran the Django unit tests with `python manage.py test scheduler` and all scheduler tests passed (`OK`, 3 tests ran).

items.body:
# 重构 PR #802 全量改动总结

## PR 概况

- **分支**: `feat/remove_cpp_device`
- **fork 点**: `228dae886` (origin/main)
- **规模**: github-opensource 589 文件 +13328/-49514；internal_source 39 文件 +289/-1348
- **核心目标**: 消除自定义 Buffer/Device/TrackerAllocator 抽象层，统一使用 torch::Tensor + ExecutionContext 单例

---

## 一、已完成的阶段

### 阶段零：删除 GptModel 类及相关死代码 ✅

- 创建 ModelTypes.h + ModelBase 接口
- 吸收 GptModel 方法到 PyWrappedModel
- 删除 GptModel 及所有派生类
- 清理死代码 layer/op 实现文件

### 阶段一：解耦 PyWrappedModel 与 Device ✅

- PyWrappedModel 简单 Buffer 操作改 torch
- DeviceProperties 解耦
- 通信/计算 Op 抽离

### 阶段二：CudaGraph 迁移 ✅

- CudaGraph 文件从 `devices/cuda_impl/` 迁移到 `rtp_llm/cpp/cuda/CudaGraph/`
- 测试迁移到 `rtp_llm/cpp/cuda/CudaGraph/tests/`

### 阶段三：删除 devices/ 目录 + Device 类层级 ✅

- 3.1-3.7: OpData/DeviceBase/DeviceOps/CudaDevice/utils/testing 迁移，devices/ 目录删除
- 3.8: Device 类层级彻底删除 + 全面消除 Device 命名

**3.8 详细改动：**

| 子任务 | 改动 |
|--------|------|
| 删除死文件 | DeviceOps.h/.cc, DeviceBase.h/.cc, CudaDevice.h/.cc, CudaDeviceRegister.cc, DeviceExport.h/.cc, DeviceFactory.h/.cc |
| DeviceFactory → ExecutionContext | initDevices→initExecCtx, getDeviceExporter→getExporter, registerExecCtxOps 移入 ExecutionContext |
| 符号重命名 | DeviceExporter→ExecCtxExporter, DeviceEvent→AsyncEvent, DeviceHook→CommHook, DeviceStream→ExecStream |
| pybind 重命名 | init_device→init_exec_ctx, get_device→get_exec_ctx, DeviceExporter→ExecCtxExporter（已加 deprecated 别名） |

### 阶段四：I/O 结构体 BufferPtr → torch::Tensor ✅

- 4.1-4.20: SamplerInputs/Output/GptModelOutputs/GptModelInputs、GenerateStream、StreamUpdateInfo、Sampler、LogitsProcessors、GenerateOutput、GenerateInput、ModelRequest、GreedyParams、通信结构体、EmbeddingInput、LoraManager、PyWrappedModel 全部转为 torch::Tensor
- W.1-W.3: Weights.h 字段全部改为 torch::Tensor（DenseWeights 含 kernel/bias/scales/zeros）
- BufferManager 内联到 DeviceBase 并删除
- 独立 Op 提取（computeLoss, cudaSoftmaxInplace, cudaMaskLogits）
- 所有 device_->clone()/select()/allocateBuffer() 消除
- Buffer/QBuffer 基础设施和 C++ Lora 代码全部删除（commit 3290a18b5）

### TrackerAllocator 删除 ✅

- commit `6eddee4c0`: 删除 TrackerAllocator, MemoryTracker, TorchCudaAllocator
- GPU 显存分配全部走 PyTorch 原生 CUDACachingAllocator
- CudaGraph pool 由 PyTorch at::cuda::CUDAGraph 原生管理

---

## 二、Review 修复（commits 9a305635d ~ 8db4b44a8）

### P0 修复

| 问题 | 修复 | Commit |
|------|------|--------|
| OpStatus 构造函数忽略 error_type 参数 | `error_type(OpErrorType::ERROR_NONE)` → `error_type(error_type)` | bcf2c36bf |
| batchCopy() need_fallback 未初始化（UB） | `bool need_fallback;` → `bool need_fallback = false;` | 8db4b44a8 |

### P1 修复

| 问题 | 修复 | Commit |
|------|------|--------|
| getExecProperties() TOCTOU 竞态 | `static ExecProperties*` → C++11 magic static `static ExecProperties prop = [this]{...}();` | 9a305635d |
| pybind API 无向后兼容别名 | 添加 deprecated 别名 get_device/init_device/DeviceExporter | 9a305635d |
| cudaMemGetInfo 返回值未检查（3处） | 添加 check_cuda_value/ROCM_CHECK | 9a305635d |
| global_instance_ 非 atomic | `ExecutionContext*` → `std::atomic<ExecutionContext*>` | 8db4b44a8 |
| setenv/getenv 跨 .so 共享裸指针 | 改为 C++17 `static inline` 成员变量，删除 env var hack（40行） | 089af82b2 |

### 其他修复

| 问题 | 修复 | Commit |
|------|------|--------|
| eplb_stream_ 未初始化 | `cudaStream_t eplb_stream_;` → `= nullptr` | 9a305635d |
| residual_scale_ 类型转换 bug | 构造时 apply `dataTypeToTorchType()` 转换 | 9a305635d |
| setGptModel 命名 | 重命名为 setModel（无调用方） | 9a305635d |
| planMicroBatches format string | `"context_batch_size"` → `"context_batch_size %ld"` | 9a305635d |

### Review 中的非代码问题说明

| 问题 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| LoRA 功能删除 | C++ Lora 代码删除，但 Python `rtp_llm/lora/lora_manager.py` 完整存在，功能未丢失 |
| --device_reserve_memory_bytes 删除 | Python argparse 中已无此参数，代码中仅剩一行注释 |
| CACHED_HOST_BUF → torch::empty().pin_memory() 性能 | 需 benchmark 验证，非代码阻塞项 |
| ExecutionContext 类过于庞大 | 后续优化项，不阻塞本 PR |

---

## 三、跨平台适配

### ROCm 适配

| 文件 | 改动 |
|------|------|
| `rtp_llm/cpp/rocm/BUILD` | 依赖路径更新 |
| `rtp_llm/cpp/rocm/custom_ar/quick_ar_comm.cc` | 适配新 API |
| `rtp_llm/cpp/rocm_compat/BUILD` | 新增 ROCm 兼容 placeholder |
| `models_py/bindings/rocm/FusedRopeKVCacheOp.cc/.h` | DeviceFactory→ExecutionContext，使用 kv_cache_kernel_block_id |
| `models_py/bindings/rocm/Gemm.cc` | 适配新 API（-198行重构） |
| `models_py/bindings/rocm/PagedAttn.cc/.h` | DeviceFactory→ExecutionContext |
| `models_py/bindings/rocm/CKAttnUtils.h` | 新增（+83行） |
| `models_py/bindings/rocm/atrexPA.cc/.h` | 新增（+514行） |
| `rtp_llm/cpp/cache/MemoryEvaluationHelper.cc` | 添加 `#include "hip_host_utils.h"` + ROCM_CHECK |
| `rtp_llm/cpp/core/ExecutionContext.cc` | ROCm 路径用 ROCM_CHECK 包裹 hipMemGetInfo |

**ROCm 团队仍需关注：**
- ROCm bindings 中如有 `dynamic_cast<ROCmDevice*>` 需自行重新设计
- ExecutionContext 的 ROCm 路径（RCCL 通信等）已在 commit 47b8262b1 适配

### PPU 适配

| 文件/改动 | 说明 |
|-----------|------|
| commit `73f42a49e` | 适配 attention factory 和 arch utils 中的 PPU device 兼容性 |
| commit `fbb88df26` | ExecutionContext::getExecProperties() 中为 PPU 构建设置 DeviceType::Ppu |
| internal_source PPU 文件 | PpuAttentionOp: DeviceEvent→AsyncEvent, DeviceStream→ExecStream |
| internal_source PPU 文件 | PpuDeviceRegister: DeviceFactory.h→ExecutionContext.h |
| internal_source PPU 文件 | PpuGemmOp/PpuGroupGemmOp: DeviceStream→ExecStream |

**PPU 团队仍需关注：**
- `PpuDeviceRegister.cc` 使用 `RTP_LLM_REGISTER_DEVICE(Ppu)` 宏，该宏已随 DeviceFactory 删除
- `PpuDevice` 继承 `CudaDevice`（已删除）— 需重新设计 PPU 设备架构

### YitianARM 适配

| 改动 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| YitianDevice.h | DeviceEvent→AsyncEvent |
| YitianDevice.cc | DeviceFactory.h→ExecutionContext.h |

**YitianARM 团队仍需关注：**
- `YitianDevice` 继承 `DeviceBase`（已删除）— 需重新设计 ARM 设备架构

### internal_source 其他适配

| 模块 | 改动 |
|------|------|
| arpc (7文件) | DeviceBase*→ExecutionContext*, allocateBuffer→torch::empty |
| RdmaMessager (3文件) | 适配新头文件 |
| barex_rdma/test/BUILD | `//rtp_llm/cpp/devices:device_defs.bzl` → `//bazel:device_defs.bzl`，`devices/testing:device_test_utils` → `testing:device_test_utils`，`devices:devices_base` → `core:devices_base` |
| bazel/arch_select.bzl | `devices/cuda_impl:gpu_register` → `cuda/ops:gpu_register` |

---

## 四、main 同步适配

### kernel_tokens_per_block 功能（来自 main 的 29 文件改动）

main 分支引入了 `kernel_tokens_per_block` / `kv_cache_kernel_block_id`，将物理 KV cache block size 与 attention kernel 可见的 block 粒度分离。

已在以下位置适配：

## 五、量化指标快照（2026-03-27）

items.title: LibJS: Remove C++ compiler pipeline, use Rust exclusively
items.body:
Now that we've been running with the Rust pipeline as the default for a couple weeks and the world hasn't exploded, let's make life easier for ourselves by removing the C++ pipeline.

- Delete Parser, Lexer, AST, ASTDump, ScopeCollector, Generator, ASTCodegen, ScopedOperand, PipelineComparison (~20,600 lines)
- Remove LIBJS_CPP, LIBJS_COMPARE_PIPELINES env vars and all fallback paths
- Port EventTarget and WebDriver to compile_dynamic_function()
- Port SyntaxHighlighter and js REPL to Rust tokenizer via new rust_tokenize() FFI
- Port test262-runner parse-only check to Rust parse_program()

45 test262 early-error tests regress. These are pre-existing differences between the Rust and C++ parsers that were previously masked by the C++ fallback. Will deal with these in a later PR.

items.title: Feat: add ChipWorker execution interface, replace bindings.py

## Summary

- Introduce `ChipWorker` (C++ via nanobind) that encapsulates the 6-step runtime C API (`dlopen`, `set_device`, `init_runtime`, `enable_profiling`, `launch_runtime`, `finalize_runtime`) into `init()`/`run()`/`reset()`
- New `CallConfig` struct for per-call parameters (`block_dim`, `aicpu_thread_num`, `orch_thread_num`, `enable_profiling`)
- `code_runner.py` migrated from `bindings.py` ctypes layer to `ChipWorker`
- `kernel_compiler.py` simplified: removed dead C++ toolchain query path, uses platform map directly
- `python/bindings.py` deleted — ctypes layer fully replaced by nanobind ChipWorker
- Fixed profiling ordering bug: `enable_profiling` now called after `init_runtime` (placement new was overwriting the flag)
- Fixed CI `detect-changes`: now catches shared code changes (`src/common/`, `python/`, `examples/scripts/`, build files) that affect A5 hardware tests

## Testing

- [x] 113 unit tests pass (`pytest tests -m "not requires_hardware"`)

- [x] a2a3sim host_build_graph: 4/4 examples pass

items.body:
>This PR is additive only: it introduces new files without changing any existing ones.

# swiGLU PTO-ISA vs torch_npu

## msprof on simulator
|PTO-ISA|torch_npu|
|---------|-----------|
|![PTO-ISA](https://github.com/Mocchibird/pto-kernels-plots/blob/main/swiglu/swiglu_pto-isa.png?raw=true)|![torch_npu](https://github.com/Mocchibird/pto-kernels-plots/blob/main/swiglu/swiglu_torch.png?raw=true)|

## msprof on device
|PTO-ISA|torch_npu|
|---------|-----------|
|![PTO-ISA](https://github.com/Mocchibird/pto-kernels-plots/blob/main/swiglu/swiglu_pto-isa_on_device.png?raw=true)|![torch_npu](https://github.com/Mocchibird/pto-kernels-plots/blob/main/swiglu/swiglu_torch_on_device.png?raw=true)|

PTO-ISA is around `24% faster` than torch_npu for simulated profiling and `13% faster`for on device profiling.

## python benchmark
|Speedup jit-compiler/ctypes vs torch_npu|Speedup cmake/pybind vs torch_npu|
|-----------|----|
|![Speedup ctypes](https://github.com/Mocchibird/pto-kernels-plots/blob/main/swiglu/swiglu_speedup_heatmap_bd24.png?raw=true)|![Speedup pybind](https://github.com/Mocchibird/pto-kernels-plots/blob/main/swiglu/swiglu_speedup_heatmap_bd24_pybind.png?raw=true)|

The benchmark with pybind has `1.5x` the speed of torch_npu, while the benchmark with jit-compiler has `0.78x` the speed of torch_npu.
Most of the slowdown for the jit-compiler version comes from repeated stream_ptr lookup. Caching the stream_ptr speeds-up the jit-compiler version significantly for smaller shapes, but not much for larger shapes.

|Speedup jit-compiler/ctypes w. cached stream_ptr vs torch_npu|

items.title: Phase 4: Add ctypes C ABI FFI support with dlopen/dlsym
items.body:
## Summary

This PR implements Phase 4 of the PyX compiler: support for calling C library functions via Python's `ctypes` module. The compiler now recognizes `ctypes.CDLL()`, `ctypes.CFUNCTYPE()`, and function pointer binding/call patterns, lowering them to LLVM IR with proper `dlopen`/`dlsym` runtime calls and type coercion between PyX and C ABI types.

## Key Changes

### Type System (`pyx/type_system.py`)
- Added `CDLL_TYPE` constant for dynamic library handles
- Added ctypes type classification: `CTYPES_INT_TYPES`, `CTYPES_FLOAT_TYPES`, `CTYPES_PTR_TYPES`, `CTYPES_ALL_TYPES`
- Implemented `is_cfuncptr_type()` to recognize internal `cfuncptr(...)` type strings
- Implemented `parse_cfuncptr_type()` to extract return and argument types from cfuncptr signatures
- Implemented `ctypes_to_pyx_type()` to map ctypes types back to PyX types (e.g., `c_int` → `int`)
- Updated `is_supported_type()` to recognize `CDLL_TYPE` and cfuncptr types

### Project Module Loading (`pyx/project.py`)
- Added `BUILTIN_MODULES` frozenset to identify standard library modules (e.g., `ctypes`)
- Modified module import tracking to handle built-in modules without requiring local `.py` files

### Analyzer (`pyx/analyzer.py`)
- Added ctypes FFI pattern recognition in `_infer_call_type()`
- Implemented `_is_ctypes_call()` to detect `ctypes.CDLL()` and `ctypes.CFUNCTYPE()` calls
- Implemented `_resolve_ctypes_type()` to extract ctypes type names from AST nodes
- Implemented `_infer_cdll_call()` to type-check `CDLL(name)` calls
- Implemented `_infer_cfunctype_call()` to type-check `CFUNCTYPE(rettype, *argtypes)` calls
- Implemented `_infer_cfuncptr_call()` to type-check indirect function pointer calls
- Added validation for ctypes argument types and function pointer signatures

### Compiler (`pyx/compiler.py`)
- Added `CTYPES_LLVM_MAP` dictionary mapping ctypes type names to LLVM IR primitive types
- Updated `llvm_type()` to handle `CDLL_TYPE` and cfuncptr types as opaque pointers
- Added `uses_dlopen` and `uses_dlsym` flags to track runtime function declarations
- Updated `emit_preamble()` to declare `@dlopen` and `@dlsym` when needed
- Implemented `_is_ctypes_call()` to recognize ctypes function calls
- Implemented `_resolve_ctypes_type_name()` to extract ctypes type names from expressions
- Implemented `_is_cfuncptr_binding()` to detect `fn_type(("symbol", lib))` pattern
- Implemented `_is_cfuncptr_call()` to detect indirect function pointer invocations
- Implemented `_compile_cdll()` to emit `dlopen(name, RTLD_LAZY)` calls
- Implemented `_compile_cfunctype()` to handle type descriptors (no runtime code)
- Implemented `_compile_cfuncptr_binding()` to emit `dlsym(lib, symbol)` calls
- Implemented `_compile_cfuncptr_call()` to emit indirect calls with proper type coercion
- Implemented `_coerce_to_ctype()` and `_coerce_from_ctype()` for int/float width conversions (trunc/sext/fptrunc/fpext)

## Notable Implementation Details

- **Type Coercion**: Integer arguments are truncated (i64 → i32) and results sign-extended (i32 → i64) when calling C functions expecting narrower types. Float arguments use `

items.body:
Originally I implemented the IDs as strings to make working with the TCL interpreter easier. This causes some performance issues since these strings need to be copied and passed around all over the place. Especially for larger circuits, this can add up.

I have migrated all of the object IDs to integers. This required updating the TCL interface to ensure that the IDs become a unique TCL string which cannot act like a integer. We do this by adding a prefix to the TCL strings to keep them unique.

items.title: Add optional C DSA core with ctypes adapter and integrate into scheduler
items.body:
### Motivation

- Improve performance and provide a native implementation for scheduling ordering and simulation by moving hot-path DSA logic into a C runtime.
- Make the C core optional and safe by providing a thin `ctypes` adapter and automatic Python fallback so existing behavior is preserved if the shared library is missing.

### Description

- Add a `c_core` native module including `include/scheduler_dsa.h`, `src/scheduler_dsa.c`, `tests/test_scheduler_dsa.c`, a `Makefile`, and a `.gitignore`, plus a convenience `run_with_c_core.sh` to build, test, migrate, and run the app.
- Implement C functions `ccore_order_indices`, `ccore_average_wait`, and `ccore_simulate_strategy` and expose them via the header for use by Python.
- Add a thin Python adapter `scheduler/c_core_adapter.py` that loads `libscheduler_dsa.so` with `ctypes`, exposes `order_tasks` and `simulate_strategy`, and falls back to existing Python implementations on error.
- Integrate the adapter into the scheduler by replacing Python-only ordering/simulation calls in `scheduler/core_classes.py` and `scheduler/data_structures.py` to call the C core via the adapter, and update `README.md` with build/run instructions and integration notes.

### Testing

- Built the shared library with `make -C c_core` and ran the C unit tests with `make -C c_core test`, which executed `c_core/tests/test_scheduler_dsa` and reported success (`PASS: c_core scheduler DSA tests`).
- The adapter is designed to fall back to the original Python implementations when the shared library is not present or fails to load, so existing Python runtime paths remain available for other automated tests.

items.title: Improvements to SWIG typemaps for Python interface
items.body:
Various improvements to the SWIG typemaps for the Python interface, organized by priority.

### Critical — Crash/Segfault Bugs

**1. Missing NULL checks in callback wrappers (`meep.i`)**

`py_callback_wrap` (line 142), `py_amp_func_wrap` (line 152), and `custom_py_src_time::dipole` (`meep-python.hpp:32`) do not check the return value of `PyObject_CallFunctionObjArgs`. If the Python callback raises an exception, these dereference NULL →segfault. Same issue in `mpb.i` callback wrappers.

**2. Use-after-free in `_get_dft_array` (`meep.i:456`)**

The `rank == 0` zero-data path passes a stack-allocated `d[1]` to `PyArray_SimpleNewFromData`, which creates a NumPy array pointing to stack memory that goes out of scope. The singleton path also leaks `dft_arr`.

### High Priority — Correctness

**3. `meep::abort()` instead of Python exceptions (`typemap_utils.cpp`)**

Many conversion functions (e.g., `pyabsorber_to_absorber`, `pymaterial_grid_to_material_grid`, `py_list_to_gobj_list`) call `abort_with_stack_trace()` on invalid input. This kills the process instead of raising a catchable Python exception. These should set `PyErr_SetString` and return an error code.

**4. `PyFunction_Check` vs `PyCallable_Check` (`meep.i:1408`)**

The `kpoint_func` typemap rejects lambdas, bound methods, `functools.portal`, and any callable that isn't a plain function. Should use `PyCallable_Check`.

### Medium Priority — Maintenance & Compatibility

**5. Dupilcate typemaps**
- `xtics/ytics/ztics` typemaps appear twice (lines 1001-1018 and 1083-1105), first block is dead code.
- `freearg` for amplitude function appears twice (lines 748 and 1069).
- `double*` array input typemaps repeat the same `is_array` + `array_data` pattern ~8 times - could use `%apply`.

### Low Priority — Cleanup

**6. Duplicate `%ignore` directives (`meep.i:1525-1529`)**

`is_variable`, `is_file`, `is_medium` each ignored twice.

items.title: Remove Qbs build system from Qt Creator 4.13.3
items.body:
Complete removal of Qbs (Qt Build Suite) from the Qt Creator 4.13.3 source tree, following the plan in `qt-creator-4.13.3-remove-qbs-detailed-steps.md`.

## Bulk deletions (~1460 files)
- All 1403 `.qbs` build files across the source tree
- `qbs/` — Qbs build modules (imports/modules)
- `src/shared/qbs/` — embedded Qbs source code
- `src/plugins/qbsprojectmanager/` — 47-file plugin
- Qbs docs (2 qdoc), screenshots (6 png), QML type descriptions (3 files)

## Build config (`.pro`/`.pri`)
- `qtcreator.pro` — removed Qbs DISTFILES and the entire `exists(src/shared/qbs/qbs.pro) { ... }` cache/doc block
- `qtcreator.pri` — removed `.qbs` auto-DISTFILES logic
- `src/shared/shared.pro` — gutted all Qbs SUBDIRS
- `src/plugins/plugins.pro` — removed conditional `qbsprojectmanager` compilation
- `autotest_dependencies.pri`, `clangtools_dependencies.pri` — dropped `qbsprojectmanager` from `QTC_TEST_DEPENDS`
- `share/share.pro`, `translations.pro` — removed Qbs paths

## C++ source (~26 files)
- Removed `Dialect::QmlQbs` enum value from `qmljsconstants.h`, `qmljsdialect.h/.cpp`
- Removed `QBS_MIMETYPE` constant and all consumers (`qmljstoolsconstants.h`, `qmljsmodelmanager.cpp`, `qmljstoolssettings.cpp`, `qmljseditor.cpp`, `cpaster/protocol.cpp`, `qmlpreviewplugin.cpp`)
- Removed `QbsRunConfigurationFactory` from `desktoprunconfiguration.h/.cpp` and `projectexplorer.cpp`
- Removed `defaultQbsBundle()` from `qmljsbundleprovider.h/.cpp`
- Removed Qbs-specific code paths in `qmljsbind.cpp`, `qmljscheck.cpp`, `qmljslink.cpp`, `qmljsimportdependencies.cpp`
- Removed `HAVE_QBS` conditional include in `androidplugin.cpp`
- Removed `.qbs` filter in `testcodeparser.cpp` and Qbs test rows in `autotestunittests.cpp`, `clangtoolsunittests.cpp`
- Removed Qbs MIME type definition from `QmlJSTools.json.in`

## Wizard templates (10 `wizard.json` files)
- Removed `QbsFile`/`QbsFileName` key definitions, Qbs BuildSystem option, `.qbs` generators, and simplified `ProjectFile` ternary expressions

## Scripts & docs
- `deployqtHelper_mac.sh` — removed qbs app deployment block
- `createSourcePackages.py` — removed qbs submodule
- `hasCopyright.pl` — removed `.qbs` exclusion
- `README.md` — removed Qbs dependency and `QBS_INSTALL_DIR` instructions
- 20+ `.qdoc`/`.qdocinc` files — removed Qbs references, fixed page navigation links

## Intentionally preserved
- `userfileaccessor.cpp` — version migration strings (no compile dependency)
- `qbswap` in `perfunwind.cpp` — Qt byte-swap function, unrelated to Qbs
- `dist/changes-*` — historical changelogs
- Third-party syntax-highlighting patch
- Comments in `resourcehandler.cpp`, `modelsmanager.cpp`, `compileroptionsbuilder.cpp`

items.body:
This PR is the same as #21415, but fixes more impacted targets (`tegra` & `sunxi`) which were missed during that PR.

---

When building on current Debian stable (13), which has a newer swig version, the build process fails. This was previously described in #20620, #21415, and in this PR's commit messages.

I was not able to find backportable commits for these targets (`tegra` & `sunxi`), as most simply updated to/past 25.01 on `main`, before this problem showed up.

For additional context, u-boot 25.01 added the SWIG fix: u-boot/u-boot@a63456b9191fae2fe49f4b121e025792022e3950, which was part of v2025.01-rc2.

As such, targets either updated to/past u-boot 25.01 (e.g. tegra 92cd360aacd697f81f649ef78a7a5bb0d007f84a and sunxi 2aba2b32ce69556be76e428499abb39293f0a94b), or removed the patch when bumping the version (e.g. rockchip 92814fec77fe24a68ab4a2acbf3ac245163c6090).
